benchmark: Darwin evolve() — DeterministicMutator vs OpenRouterMutator (PR #37 src)
method: Ran packages/darwin-mode/dist/evolve.js on a real target repo profile (/tmp/darwin-target: package.json + src + README), once with the default DeterministicMutator and once with the OpenRouterMutator (anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5) injected via EvolutionConfig.generator. Same seed (7), generations 1, childrenPerGeneration 2, concurrency 2, promotionDelta 0.05. finalScore is the PR's own immutable scorer (src/scorer.ts, ADR-072/075/076). Real OpenRouter calls.

model_routing_optimization.method: Same evolve() run, mutator routed to different model tiers. finalScore is identical (ceiling 0.985) so the only measurable axis is cost+latency.
model_routing_optimization.delta: Routing the LLM mutator from haiku-4.5 to gemini-2.5-flash: identical 0.985 outcome at 2.26× lower cost ($0.002662 vs $0.006007) and faster (5.0s vs 6.5s). This is the real, on-instrument optimization delta — a cost reduction at zero quality cost, since the scorer is ceiling-bound.

finding: The LLM mutator and the deterministic mutator reach the SAME winner finalScore (0.985). This is not a null result — it is structural. The scorer (ADR-072) is a correctness/safety GATE, not a quality discriminator: weights are taskSuccess 0.35 + testPassRate 0.20 + traceQuality 0.15 + costEfficiency 0.10 + latencyEfficiency 0.10 + safetyScore 0.10. ADR-075 stubs costEfficiency and latencyEfficiency to 1.0 for reproducibility, and traceQuality binary-caps at 0.9. So ANY safe, test-passing variant lands at 0.35 + 0.20 + 0.135 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.10 = 0.985. No mutator — deterministic or frontier LLM — can exceed this ceiling. 'Beyond SOTA' is therefore NOT expressible as an evolve finalScore delta.
beyond_sota_reconciliation: Because the evolve quality outcome is ceiling-bounded and identical, the real optimization is COST: routing the LLM mutator to the cheap tier (gemini-2.5-flash / haiku-4.5) yields the SAME 0.985 evolve outcome at ~1/42 the per-call cost of a frontier mutator. The DRACO frontier (draco-quality-cost-frontier.json) independently confirms the cheap model's *output* quality is equal-or-higher (gemini-2.5-flash 82 > opus-4 76), so the cost saving carries no quality penalty. Recommendation for ADR-072/075: state explicitly that evolve's finalScore is a gate, and route the mutator to the cheap tier by default.
